G4OCCTSolid: reuse cached BRepAdaptor_Surface in DistanceToOut normal query (#212)

* G4OCCTSolid: reuse cached BRepAdaptor_Surface in DistanceToOut normal query

TryGetOutwardNormal was constructing BRepAdaptor_Surface(face) on every
call from DistanceToOut(p,v,...).  In the B4c profile this accumulates to
1,682M instructions (10.1% of total) across 246k calls.

FaceBounds already stores a BRepAdaptor_Surface adaptor per face, built
once in ComputeBounds().  Add a primary TryGetOutwardNormal overload that
takes a const BRepAdaptor_Surface& directly (moving the existing body
there, minus the adaptor construction).  Keep the original TopoDS_Face
overload as a one-line shim for the SurfaceNormal path (~84 calls).

In DistanceToOut(p,v,...) look up intersector.Face(minIndex) in
fFaceBoundsCache via TopoDS_Shape::IsSame() (O(1) TShape pointer
compare) and pass the cached adaptor to the new overload.  A fallback
to on-the-fly construction is retained for any face not found in the
cache (should not occur in practice).

Expected impact: ~10% reduction in instruction count for workloads
dominated by DistanceToOut(p,v) normal queries (B4c calorimeter example).

commit.message:
Fix heredoc delimiter quoting: apply quote-removal before matching closing line (#320)

The flash lexer stores heredoc delimiter words verbatim (e.g. `<<'EOF'`
→ `HereDoc("'EOF'")`), but bash's closing delimiter is the result of
quote removal on that word — just `EOF`. The dparser was comparing raw
closing-line tokens against the unstripped delimiter, so quoted heredocs
never closed.

## Changes

- **`src/dparser.rs`** — adds `strip_heredoc_delimiter_quotes(delim)`
implementing POSIX quote-removal:
  - Single-quoted spans: `'FOO'` → `FOO`
- Double-quoted spans with inner backslash handling per POSIX (`$`, `` `
``, `"`, `\`, newline are special; others keep the backslash)
- Unquoted backslash escapes: `\X` → `X`; lone trailing `\` kept
literally
  - Mixed/partial quoting: `E'O'F` → `EOF`
- Applied at the `HereDoc`/`HereDocDash` match arm before pushing to the
heredocs queue

- **`src/command_acceptance.rs`** — adds
`test_here_documents_quoted_delimiter` covering all four quoting forms
(`'…'`, `"…"`, `\`, mixed) and the `<<-` dash variant

- **`src/dparser.rs` tests** — adds
`test_strip_heredoc_delimiter_quotes` (unit) and per-form annotation
tests verifying `IsOpening`/`IsClosing` pairing

```bash
# Previously: heredoc never closed, will_bash_accept_buffer returned false
cat <<'EOF'
hello
EOF        # ← closing line didn't match "'EOF'" stored in queue
```

commit.message:
Fix GitHub Pages 404s for reports and missing callgrind links in bench report (#207)

* Initial plan

* Fix GitHub Pages 404s and missing callgrind links in bench report

Three issues fixed in ci.yml:

1. Download ci-reports to docs/ (not docs/reports/) in deploy job.
   The artifact is uploaded with two paths (reports/ and bench_raw.json),
   so GitHub Actions preserves the reports/ prefix in the artifact.
   Downloading to docs/reports/ caused double-nesting at
   docs/reports/reports/... (404 for point_cloud_viewer.html and
   test-results.md). Downloading to docs/ maps reports/ correctly to
   docs/reports/.

2. Add family field to each callgrind matrix fixture so the
   family/id key format (e.g. direct-primitives/g4box-box-20x30x40-v1)
   matches what generate_bench_report.py uses when looking up callgrind
   links. Previously only the bare id was used as the key, causing
   callgrind_links.get(fixture_id) to always return None.

3. Update callgrind_links JSON key from bare id to family/id format.

Add fuzzy case/accent-insensitive name matching for OCR member identification

Replace all 6 exact-substring name comparisons with _names_match() which:
- Normalizes to lowercase and strips accents (é→e, ô→o, etc.)
- Checks substring containment first
- Falls back to SequenceMatcher fuzzy ratio (≥80% threshold)

Add callgrind-b4c CI job to profile G4OCCTSolid navigation call distribution (#203)

* Add callgrind-b4c CI job to profile G4OCCTSolid navigation call distribution

B4c uses G4OCCTSolid for the absorber and gap shapes (STEP-imported), making
it the ideal vehicle for profiling realistic G4OCCTSolid call patterns across
all five navigation methods: Inside(p), DistanceToIn(p,v), DistanceToOut(p,v),
DistanceToIn(p) [safety], DistanceToOut(p) [safety].

Changes:

src/examples/B4c/CMakeLists.txt:
- Add option(B4C_CHECK_OVERLAPS ON): allows CI to pass OFF to prevent placement
  overlap checking from inflating Inside() call counts during construction.
- target_compile_definitions: B4C_CHECK_OVERLAPS=<1|0> passed to the compiler
  so DetectorConstruction.hh can use it as a default member initializer.
- if(VALGRIND_INCLUDE_DIR): add HAVE_VALGRIND_CALLGRIND_H + include path for
  exampleB4c, enabling CALLGRIND macros in RunAction.

src/examples/B4c/include/DetectorConstruction.hh:
- fCheckOverlaps default initializer changed from true to B4C_CHECK_OVERLAPS
  so the CI profiling job disables it at compile time without touching source.

src/examples/B4c/src/RunAction.cc:
- Add CALLGRIND guard block (HAVE_VALGRIND_CALLGRIND_H) at top.
- BeginOfRunAction: CALLGRIND_START_INSTRUMENTATION + CALLGRIND_TOGGLE_COLLECT
  -> geometry construction and physics init excluded from profile.
- EndOfRunAction:   CALLGRIND_TOGGLE_COLLECT + CALLGRIND_STOP_INSTRUMENTATION.

src/examples/B4c/run_profile.mac (new):
- 100 events, e- 300 MeV, /run/verbose 0.  Enough statistics for stable
  per-method call ratios; with --instr-atstart=no, runtime is manageable.

.github/workflows/ci.yml:
- New callgrind-b4c job: builds exampleB4c with B4C_CHECK_OVERLAPS=OFF and
  VALGRIND_INCLUDE_DIR, runs run_profile.mac under callgrind with gated
  instrumentation, annotates with callgrind_annotate, uploads artifact
  callgrind-reports-b4c (annotated txt + raw .out, 7-day retention).

commit.message:
fix(forward-spectrometer-v1): correct 1250 mm z-shift between STEP and GDML geometries (#191)

* Initial plan

* fix: correct forward-spectrometer STEP z-shift (centre geometry at world origin)

The STEP geometry was at absolute detector z=[0,2400] mm while the GDML
world is centred at z=0 (spanning [-1250,+1250] mm), causing a 1250 mm
z-offset between the two representations and many benchmark mismatches.

- generate.tcl: subtract 1250 mm from all z positions so shapes are
  created in the world frame (z=-1250 to +1150 mm)
- shape.step: shift all 3D CARTESIAN_POINT z-values by -1250 mm
- geometry.gdml: update comments to describe positions in world-frame
  terms (remove confusing "absolute detector z minus 1250 mm" note)
- provenance.yaml: document world-frame z positions and add
  coordinate_frame: world metadata

  positions for all components (was still citing detector-absolute

commit.message:
Replace faceted STEP approximations with exact analytic surfaces for G4Hype and G4Paraboloid (#189)

* Initial plan

* fix: replace faceted lofts with exact analytic NURBS for G4Hype and G4Paraboloid

Replace the 24-segment faceted circular loft STEP files for G4Hype and
G4Paraboloid with exact analytic NURBS solids built from true conic
sections, and remove the corresponding xfail.

The old approach used polyline sections through profile samples fed into
thrusections. The resulting polyhedral surfaces were fundamentally
incompatible with the sub-nanometre ray-comparison tolerance.

New approach (DRAWEXE):
- G4Paraboloid: parabola() with focal F=k/4=1.8 (from r²=7.2·(z+25)),
  trim to t∈[6,18], revol 360° around Z, sew + mkvolume + nurbsconvert.
- G4Hype: hyperbola() with a=8, b=8/tan(20°), trim to u∈[±asinh(25/b)],
  revol 360° around Z, sew + mkvolume + nurbsconvert.

nurbsconvert converts the SURFACE_OF_REVOLUTION (PARABOLA/HYPERBOLA)
to degree-(2,2) RATIONAL_B_SPLINE_SURFACE — the same format used by
the G4Ellipsoid fixture — which the OCCT STEPControl_Reader can read.

The resulting surfaces are geometrically exact: ray comparisons pass at
the Geant4 surface tolerance (≈0.5×10⁻⁹ mm). Volume expectations are
updated to the analytic values; tolerance is 1.0 mm³ to accommodate
BRepGProp Gauss-quadrature integration variance over NURBS.

commit.message:
Remove 117 redundant tests identified by coverage fingerprinting

Per-test instrumented coverage analysis revealed that 715 of 855 tests
cover zero unique Rust source lines. Within that set, 119 tests share
identical coverage fingerprints with another test in the same class —
meaning they exercise the exact same code paths with trivially different
inputs.

Two tests (get_missing_with_default for Document and DictProxy) were
restored after discovering they cover a distinct closure/region within
a shared line — a case where line-level coverage misses sub-line
branching.

Methodology:
- Built instrumented .so with RUSTFLAGS="-Cinstrument-coverage"
- Ran each of 855 tests individually, collecting per-test (file, line) sets
- Grouped tests by identical coverage fingerprint
- Within each same-class group, kept one representative and removed the rest
- Verified: 738 tests pass, line coverage 98.38% (unchanged)

commit.message:
fix: use path-agnostic gcovr filters to fix zero coverage report (#190)

The previous filter anchored on the absolute path returned by
realpath(1) inside the Singularity container:

  --filter "${ROOT}/src/G4OCCT.*"
  --filter "${ROOT}/include/G4OCCT/.*"

Inside the eic-shell Apptainer container the workspace is accessible
via a bind-mount whose path may not round-trip through realpath(1)
identically to the path that cmake (which calls realpath(3) internally
via cmSystemTools::CollapseFullPath) records in .gcno files.  When the
two diverge, gcovr emits "All coverage data is filtered out" and the
report shows 0 / 0 for every metric.

Replace the absolute-path filters with path-agnostic patterns that
start with / (so gcovr treats them as absolute-path regexes) but use
.* to absorb whatever prefix the build system used:

  --filter '/.*/src/G4OCCT[^/]*'
  --filter '/.*/include/G4OCCT/.*'

These match project library source and header files regardless of the
concrete absolute path in use inside the container.  The --root option
(which only controls how paths are displayed in the HTML report, not
which files are included) continues to use realpath(1).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Per-fixture process isolation for assembly GDML/STEP benchmarks (#187)

* Initial plan

* Fix barrel-tracker-v1 geometry.gdml to use unique volume and material names

Both barrel-tracker-v1 and triple-box-v1 used identical names for their
world logical volume ("WorldLV"), world solid ("WorldBox"), and air material
("G4_AIR"). When G4GDMLParser::GetWorldVolume() searches the global Geant4
stores by name, it could return triple-box's world for barrel-tracker's
benchmark, causing it to compare cylinders (STEP) against boxes (GDML).

Rename to fixture-unique names:
  - "WorldBox"  → "BarrelTrackerWorldBox"
  - "WorldLV"   → "BarrelTrackerWorldLV"
  - "G4_AIR"    → "BT_AIR"  (also avoids NIST material-name conflicts)
  - element "N" → "BT_N"
  - element "O" → "BT_O"
  - setup world ref updated to "BarrelTrackerWorldLV"
  - provenance.yaml updated to mention "BT_AIR"

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/wdconinc/G4OCCT/sessions/15e35f41-3799-4a5e-b3ff-3196b76e3127

Run bench_assembly_navigator once per fixture so each gets a clean Geant4
global state (material table, solid store, logical-volume store), preventing
cross-fixture name conflicts without requiring workarounds in fixture files.

  and shape.step, treat it as the fixture dir (not a parent to scan). CI
  invokes the binary once per fixture dir in this mode.
- Extract RegisterFixture lambda with explicit captures for clarity.
- Update docstring to document both single-fixture and multi-fixture modes.

ci.yml:
- Replace single all-fixtures invocation with a for-loop over fixture dirs.
- Each invocation writes bench_assembly_raw_<fixture_id>.json.
- Per-fixture failures warn but continue (benchmarks are not gatekeepers).

generate_assembly_report.py:
- Accept a glob pattern as first arg (bench_assembly_raw_*.json).
- Expand the glob and merge benchmark entries from all matching JSON files.
- Single-file usage (literal path) remains backward compatible.

barrel-tracker-v1/geometry.gdml:

commit.message:
feat: index title + og:description for richer semantic embeddings

Previously only the article title was embedded. Now each document is
indexed as 'Title. Description' using the og:description meta tag,
which contains a meaningful summary of the article content.

This significantly improves semantic search recall — queries about
concepts mentioned only in the article body (not the title) now match
correctly. Existing indexes should be rebuilt with build-index.

Also includes entity decoding for meta tag content (& etc.) and
expanded Range header from 8KB to 16KB to capture longer <head> sections.

commit.message:
Show agent-mode-not-configured error on Ctrl+I / Shift+Enter (#316)

Pressing Ctrl+I or Shift+Enter when agent mode isn't set up silently did
nothing. Now both keys show an `AiError` with a message directing the
user to configure it.

## Changes

- **New key handlers** in `on_keypress`: added match arms for Ctrl+I,
Alt+I, and Shift+Enter guarded by `ai_command.is_empty()` — previously
these arms only fired when `ai_command` was non-empty, leaving the
unconfigured case unhandled
- **`show_agent_mode_not_configured_error()`**: new helper that
transitions to `ContentMode::AiError` with the message:
  ```
Agent mode is not configured. Run `flyline agent-mode --help` or see
https://github.com/HalFrgrd/flyline#agent-mode
  ```
Displayed in red via the existing `AiError` rendering path; dismissed
with Escape or Enter as usual

Fix accept/decline friend request: use setQueryData instead of invalidateQueries

The IDB offline-first cache (gcTime: 7 days) causes a race where
invalidateQueries triggers a refetch but the persisted stale data
renders before the fresh result arrives, making the UI appear frozen.

Fix: replace invalidateQueries with setQueryData for instant updates:
- acceptMutation: pass full FriendRequest to mutation; on success,
  filter it out of ['friend-requests'] and append to ['friends']
- declineMutation: on success, filter declined request from
  ['friend-requests']

Same pattern as CharacterSelectPage create/delete fix.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

  so the recipient's Requests tab automatically refreshes and shows

  to TYPE_ICON map so friend notifications display meaningful icons
